problem_statement: The temporal retention window can prove that a lifecycle action is due, but destructive or high-risk lifecycle actions also need retained approval certificate and backup guard evidence before the receipt may admit action allowance.
success_criteria: Destructive or high-risk retention actions declare retention approval as required when retention checking is active
A due destructive retention action is blocked when approval certificate refs are missing
A due destructive retention action is blocked when the approval certificate hash is missing
A due destructive retention action is blocked when the backup guard hash is missing and policy requires it
Retention-active actions remain deferred by timing without being blocked only because future approval evidence is absent
Due or overdue actions with approval refs, approval certificate hash, and backup guard hash remain schema-valid and lifecycle-allowed
Low-risk actions with retention checking disabled do not require retention approval evidence
non_goals: no live data deletion, anonymization, archive, or compaction
constraints: retention approval evidence must be part of the same hash-bound temporal retention receipt
approval requirements must not bypass legal hold, tenant scope, source receipt, or delete-after timing checks
missing approval evidence must fail closed with deterministic blocker codes
the schema must reject action-due or overdue receipts that claim approval-required allowance without approval and backup guard evidence
evidence_required: focused temporal retention approval-boundary test receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
